notes: SHIP v1.14.257 — TRAY FIDELITY (per HANDOFF-INSPECT3D-TRAY-FIDELITY.md). Implements John's 2026-07-15 ruling: INTERIOR DETAIL IS A TYPE SIGNATURE, NOT A SPEC SHEET — and fixes the .256 defect John saw on device (dense rack read as COLOURED SLABS, not the mock). ROOT CAUSE (OODA-proven, correctly attributed by the handoff to the PREVIOUS handoff's §3 text, not the executor): .256's chassis material was the mock's recipe with ONE variable swapped — color/emissive = saturated TYPE_COLOR hex — where the mock uses a DARK gunmetal per-kind palette. TWO EDITS, dct-ios.html only. EDIT A — CHASSIS PALETTE RESTORED + TYPE COLOUR DEMOTED TO ACCENT: chassis material now color/emissive = KIND_C[kind] (compute 0x1a2332 · switch 0x1e293b · power 0x1a2028 · blank 0x0f141c · patch 0x18222e · media 0x161d26 · storage 0x141c26 · generic 0x121a24), same metal/rough/opacity/env as before. TYPE colour (hex, computed EXACTLY as .256 via TYPE_COLOR[disp] — the truth channel is UNCHANGED, it just stops painting the whole box) moves to a FRONT BEZEL STRIP: a PlaneGeometry(cw*0.92, min(hT*0.14, uH*0.20)) in hex at the tray's lower front face, on EVERY non-blank tray — glance-readable honesty now lives here. UNKNOWN trays ADDITIONALLY get a magenta (0xff2bd6, opacity 0.85) EdgesGeometry outline around the chassis so magenta stays unmissable at arm's length (.251 honesty arc); the gold hgtUnknown hatch is unchanged and COEXISTS (height-unknown != type-unknown, the .246 line). Blank keeps the mock ghost (chassis recoloured 0x0a0f14, opacity 0.4, no strip). Raycast target + userData.dev + all .256 LED/detail meshes UNCHANGED. EDIT B — INTERIOR LIBRARY for the generic bucket (kills 'BLANK over real gear'): kind routing extended — patch→'patch', media→'media', storage→'storage', unknown→'generic', server stays compute (already correct). New signature interiors (dark blocks 0x050a10-0x0a1018, shared materials, InstancedMesh for the repeats, §5): PATCH = two full-width rows of 16 port squares (BoxGeometry 0.05x0.03x0.012, ONE InstancedMesh of 32) + a teal LED (0x1fffd0) on every 4th port — representative rows, NOT a port-count claim (John's ruling); MEDIA = the 1U shelf read — 6 converter bricks (BoxGeometry cw*0.12 x hT*0.55 x 0.03) each with a green status LED + a thin shelf lip at the bottom edge; STORAGE = one row of 10 vertical drive blades (BoxGeometry cw*0.07 x hT*0.6 x 0.015, ONE InstancedMesh) + a green LED per blade (the mock's compute drive-bay language at full width); GENERIC (unknown) = dark chassis + magenta strip + magenta outline (from EDIT A) and NOTHING ELSE — the old .256 generic status-LED was REMOVED; zero invented hardware, honest and loud (§3 zero-fabrication). ZERO-FABRICATION HELD: every signature is decoration keyed to TYPE, never a claim about a specific device's port/drive/brick counts. STANDING RULINGS RATIFIED by John this handoff (my .256 flags): (1) REAR FINISH VERBATIM — copper bus bars / green cartridge CONFIRMED, the side-by-side bar is the law, the §2 chrome-rear text is dead; (2) RACK EARS ON COMPUTE per the mock CONFIRMED. (John may still flip either at device-verify; until then ruled.) §5 budget: shared materials throughout (KIND_C map, tealLedMat, magOutlineMat, and a _stripMat(hex) cache keyed on the ~9 distinct type colours so bezel strips don't allocate per-tray); InstancedMesh for patch ports (32) + storage blades (10) on top of .256's drive/port instancing; no per-frame allocation added. Est. mesh delta per generic-bucket tray: patch ~32 instanced + ~8 LEDs; media ~13; storage ~10 instanced + 10 LEDs — all modest; actual count + FPS = John's device (§device-verify). Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS; CSS brace balance 3857/3857 UNCHANGED (handoff expected NO CSS — correct, the strip/outline are geometry not CSS); CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0); three-stamp lockstep .256->.257. ?legacy=1 BYTE-IDENTICAL: all edits are inside rackElevation_render3D, reachable only via the body.rd 3D toggle; the legacy house never enters it; no CSS touched. NOTED, STILL OWED (unchanged, own ships): the FLAT rack-elevation EDP-vocabulary colour bug; the reh3d_setMode `if (!host) return;` silent return (hardening ship). BATCH: this stacks on the .251-.256 batch (now .251-.257) — WELL PAST the 6-cap. John device-verified .256 (found the colour-slab defect -> this ship; ratified rear/ears) and directed this corrective ship + the EDIT-C micro-ship in one handoff drop; recorded honestly, his call. A full consolidated device pass is owed. EDIT C (site profile classifier) ships SEPARATELY as v1.14.258 per the handoff ('own stamp, own verify'). DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone — side-by-side with the mock remains the bar): [1] Dense rack in 3D: chassis read as DARK GUNMETAL hardware, NOT colour slabs — mock-equivalent at a glance. [2] Type still readable in 2s: bezel strips (cyan/violet/gold/teal/slate) POP against the dark chassis. [3] UNKNOWN: magenta strip + magenta outline unmissable; gold hatch still marks height-unknown. [4] Patch/media/storage trays show their signatures (ports / bricks / blades) — NO bare boxes over real gear anywhere. [5] A sparse rack AND a dense GPU/switch rack both look right — same renderer, any rack. [6] Scan wave, band, bay, tap-to-detail, FORGE GL guard — all unchanged. [7] FPS acceptable — note mesh count. [8] ?legacy=1 byte-identical. STILL OWED — JOHN'S RULING (data, unchanged): DFW02 empty-model row @c1:001:38; heights for gpu-b40-02(160)/cpu-gp2-*(165)/inf-med-01(60)/om2216-c14(16)/fs-media-converter-chassis(5)/VAST DBox(50)/net-ufm-05; whether .252 magenta reads too loud; the FLAT-elevation EDP-vocabulary colour bug (own ship).
